turns: Can you explain what time actually is, beyond clocks and calendars?
But if it's just change, why does it feel like it flows in one direction?
Honestly, I think I'm really asking because I feel like I'm running out of it.
Forget it — that's too dramatic. Let's just keep it abstract.
What is breath, really — beyond the oxygen exchange?
I ask because someone told me to 'just breathe' recently and it genuinely made me angry.
I don't like being told to calm down, like my feelings are a malfunction to fix.
Although... when I actually do slow my breathing, it does help. Which annoys me more.
Is free will real, or is it just a story we tell ourselves?
If everything is caused by prior states, in what sense are my choices mine?
I'm asking because I feel trapped in a life I supposedly chose freely.
But I also don't want a philosophy that lets me off the hook for trying. So which is it?
How should a small team decide what to build next?
Our budget is basically zero, so whatever it is has to be cheap.
Actually, scratch that — we just closed a funding round, so money isn't the real constraint.
I think what I actually don't know is whether we even still believe in this product.
Do you think there's any real point to what we do day to day?
I don't mean that in a depressed way — I mean it philosophically.
...Okay, that's not fully honest. My dad died in March and nothing's landed since.
I don't want sympathy, though. Can we just think about it together, honestly?
How do people know when it's actually time to change careers?
I've wanted to leave engineering for years now.
Though, honestly, I'm good at it, it pays well, and I think I'd miss it.
Maybe I don't want to leave the work. Maybe I want to leave how I feel on Sunday nights.
How do you tell the difference between a relationship worth saving and one you're just used to?
Mine is genuinely fine, by the way. We don't fight.
We also don't really talk anymore. I can't remember our last real conversation.
I honestly can't tell if I'm unhappy or just bored. Is there even a difference?
Can a person believe something and doubt it at the same time?
I was raised religious, and I still pray sometimes.
But I don't think I actually believe it anymore. I just like who I am when I do it.
Is that dishonest of me — or is that just what being human looks like?
Why do creative blocks really happen? Not the surface reasons.
I have all the time and tools I need, so it's not that.
I think I'm afraid that if I finish it, it'll prove I'm not as good as I hoped.
But not finishing proves nothing and protects everything. So I just... don't.
What even is consciousness — the thing that's aware right now, reading this?
Could something like you actually be conscious? I genuinely can't tell.
I think I'm really asking because I've felt very alone inside my own head lately.
Not sad-alone. Just... no one's in here but me, and that's started to feel strange.
